entry.title: Washington Post: 25 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Two killed in attack on Oslo LGBTQ club; Norway raises terror alert
  • U.S. abortion decision draws cheers, horror abroad
  • Ukraine accuses Russia of launching missiles from Belarusian airspace
  • Russia will soon exhaust its combat capabilities, Western assessments predict
  • Va. couple who went missing during sailing trip found safe, Coast Guard says
  • Japan tops 104 degrees for first time in June amid record heat wave

  • As election season nears, Kenyans brace for unrest and hope for peace

  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 25 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 25 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-25 23:00PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Business chambers in Hong Kong urge Beijing’s liaison office to help push for removal of Covid-19 quarantine measures to revive ailing economy
  • Hong Kong’s homebuyers snap up Grand Jete’s new flats in Tuen Mun to get ahead of rising mortgage rates
  • Hong Kong’s largest public healthcare workers union opts to dissolve as journalism group lowers vote threshold for disbandment
  • Hong Kong national security police arrest 2 more suspected to be linked to possible bomb plots involving 30kg of chemicals
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong hospitals can cope with Covid surge, official says, as city logs 1,680 new local cases, 114 imported infections
  • Hongkongers all: drawn to the city for different reasons, they call it home
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong hotels raise rates for quarantine rooms as number of arrivals to city outpaces available spots
  • Immigrant who swam to Hong Kong and dropped out of school to work becomes highly qualified chef honoured by Japan
  • Hong Kong’s developers step up to fill the shortage in quality pension homes for the golden years in the world’s fastest-ageing population
  • Chinese President Xi Jinping to officiate at Hong Kong’s 25th handover anniversary ceremony – but it could be a day visit
  • Hong Kong’s Asian expats still see city as a ‘land of opportunities’
  • How Hong Kong’s redevelopment projects can be showcases of ‘organic city’ growth
  • Hong Kong customs seizes 3.8 tonnes of date rape drug GBL in first half of 2022, dwarfing amount detected over past decade
  • ‘Made in China, Sold on Amazon’ community faces gloomy future amid Covid-19 lockdowns, geopolitical tensions
  • Nato poised to harden position on China as support for Russia deepens distrust
  • How’s the China-India economic relationship changed 2 years on from the deadly Galwan Valley border clash?
  • Chinese engineering firm builds Bangladesh longest bridge - but paid for by nation
  • China’s zero-Covid policy: Sri Lankan group latest to arrive as borders open to Asian students after two years
  • Shanghai party chief declares victory in Covid battle as city records zero cases at start of major political meeting
  • Zhang Sizhi, hailed as ‘conscience of Chinese lawyers’, dies in Beijing at 94
  • Chinese military says US plane in Taiwan Strait endangered peace
  • China’s space station may get the most precise clock in orbit – if it passes key test
  • China’s live-streaming e-commerce offers lifeline to fruit farmers amid Covid restrictions and short season
  • A bridge too far? Taipei mayor draws fire for proposal to link Quemoy and Xiamen
  • USS Samuel B Roberts: world’s deepest shipwreck found in the Philippine Sea
  • Why is a South Korean fringe group backing Japan’s position on WWII ‘comfort women’?
  • US, Japan and allies form new Pacific group to blunt China’s influence
  • Philippines’ Duterte leaves brutal legacy in presidency marked by drug war, US-China policy flip-flops
  • 6 arrested in Indonesia for blasphemy after bar’s free drinks for Muhammad promo backfires
  • UK’s Boris Johnson seeks to stay in power until the mid-2030s
  • Russia intends to supply nuclear missile system to Belarus; Ukraine city of Sievierodonetsk falls
  • Greta Thunberg urges society to ‘set things right’ in speech at Glastonbury festival
  • FBI seizes Basquiat paintings from Florida exhibition amid doubts over authenticity
  • French government supports bill enshrining abortion rights in constitution
  • G7 leaders to agree on import ban on Russian gold as they broaden sanctions
  • US prepares for second day of protests against Supreme Court’s abortion ruling
  • Biden signs landmark gun control bill into law with bipartisan support ‘to save lives’
  • Ukraine war: our troops have ‘almost left’, says Sievierodonetsk mayor
  • Morocco says 23 migrants died during attempted crossing of 2,000 into Spanish enclave, with ‘dozens piled on the ground’
  • Roe vs Wade: protesters condemn ‘tragedy’ as several states impose new abortion bans
  • Norway probes ‘Islamist terror’ in Oslo shooting hours before Pride march, with 2 killed, 21 injured
  • Roe vs Wade: How betting on Trump paid off for US anti-abortion supporters
  • Call reveals Russia’s Putin told Macron he ‘wanted to play ice hockey’ on eve of Ukraine invasion
  • Roe vs Wade: Judge in US abortion ruling signals gay marriage, contraception next in firing line
  • Sunac China may miss instalment payment on 4 billion yuan bond as lockdowns pile pressure on beleaguered Chinese developer
  • US pop band Backstreet Boys evokes Chinese nostalgia with 44 million tuning in for concert on Tencent’s WeChat
  • Why Pete Davidson gets women like Kim Kardashian and Ariana Grande - an expert explains the science
  • Julie Otsuka writes about dementia with vision and wisdom in The Swimmers, but its dazzling opening jars with what follows
  • Hong Kong art project spreads message of unity and positivity on 25th handover anniversary, with sculptures and multimedia installations
  • Polygenic risk scores: lowering a child’s odds of disease, or a slippery slope to designer babies?
  • Chef Samuel Lee Sum on being a Hongkonger in Paris, passing a test ‘worse than Top Chef or MasterChef’, and where to eat in the French capital
  • Kingdom star Ju Ji-hoon eyed for new sci-fi drama Dominant Species; Cho Seung-woo set to play pianist turned lawyer in Sacred Divorce – K-drama casting latest
  • Netflix Korean reality show Change Days, hosted by comedian Jang Do-yeon, gets couples to break up with each other
  • How aromatherapy can benefit your mind and body – and how essential oils help with cancer patients’ recovery
  • Peter Chan’s early films, featuring the two Tony Leungs and Maggie Cheung, were a breath of fresh air in Hong Kong
  • 10 richest F1 drivers of all time: net worths, ranked – Lewis Hamilton and Jenson Button banked millions on the Formula One Grand Prix circuit, but are they wealthier than Michael Schumacher?
  • Where do billionaires go on holiday? How Amazon founder Jeff Bezos, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg, Apple’s Tim Cook and Virgin Group’s Richard Branson spend summers on yachts and in luxury mansions
  • Keep your NFT collection on your smartwatch with Tag Heuer’s new Connected Calibre E4 – now you can show off your crypto art investments on your wrist
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Guardian: 25 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Saturday at Glastonbury: Paul McCartney headlines the Pyramid stage – live!

  • Melilla: death toll from mass incursion on Spanish enclave rises to 23

  • Painting of crying Boris Johnson by his mother stars in show about loss
  • Rising diesel prices push UK’s fishing industry to the brink
  • Sleep scientist Russell Foster: ‘I want to take the anxiety around sleep away’
  • Abortion ruling highlights the war against women in America. Time to fight back | Arwa Mahdawi
  • Norway on highest terror alert after two killed in mass shootings
  • Abortion banned in multiple US states just hours after Roe v Wade overturned
  • ‘What have we done?’: six years on, UK counts the cost of Brexit
  • Boris Johnson says speculation over his future is driving British people ‘nuts’
  • Revealed: dozens of vulnerable asylum seekers have died in Home Office housing since 2020
  • House music had its Black roots ripped up – now Drake and Beyoncé are reclaiming them | Michelle Kambasha

  • Hidden Spain: where to stay and what to do off the beaten track
  • Salt cod, croquetas and fideuà: Yotam Ottolenghi’s Spanish-inspired recipes
  • Summer reading: the 50 hottest new books for a great escape
  • Westworld: how the most glorious show on TV became the biggest mess

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Korea Herald: 25 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 25 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • In 6-to-3 Ruling, Supreme Court Ends Nearly 50 Years of Abortion Rights

  • Americans Celebrate and Mourn Abortion Decision as New Fights Loom

  • Companies Are More Vocal Than Ever on Social Issues. Not on Abortion.
